title: My AI workflow for turning trading ideas into backtest code
author: wealthlearn
selftext:
Anyone else feel like the hardest part of building a new trading strategy isn't the idea, it's actually getting it coded up for a proper backtest?
I used to spend hours just translating a simple concept into Pine Script or Python. Missing a bracket, wrong variable name, it all adds up. And that's before I even knew if the idea had any merit.
Lately, I've been refining a workflow that cuts this down a lot. I start with a plain English description of a strategy idea. Think 'buy when RSI crosses below 30 and MACD is bullish'. Then, I feed that into a tool like WealthLearn's QuantCodeGPT. It spits out Python or Pine Script ready for my backtester. I usually do a quick check, maybe tweak a parameter, and then run it.
This has drastically changed my iteration speed. I'm able to test 3-4 distinct strategy variations in the time it used to take me to just code one. Yesterday, I pushed through 5 different variations of a simple momentum idea in about an hour. One of them actually showed a 0.7 Sharpe over 3 years on a 1-hour BTC chart.
It's not perfect, still needs human oversight. But what's everyone else doing to speed up their strategy development process with AI?

comments.author: TacticalInvesting
comments.body: For me it was organizing the input data for my backtests. I went trough years for data manually with excel… boy, I don‘t miss that. AI can fix (or highlight) simple mistakes / problems easily

comments.author: The_AI_Trader
comments.body:
100%. But we all need to understand that trying to find the holy grail of a system that is built on 100% technical analysis, is doomed from the start. 

Instead, we should deploy AI to analyze and inject dynamic strategies , incorporating technical and fundamentals. 

That’s the way to go.
